title: CostModel
description: The realistic cost model charged before any gate sees a return. Taker fees are charged per side on every |delta position| change; financing/borrow is charged on the FULL levered/short NOTIONAL — not on 1 unit. This encodes the decisive dated-futures-basis lesson: charging the risk-free / financing rate on 1 unit while the book was ~2.95x-levered overstated net returns and collapsed once corrected (Sharpe 1.64 -> 0.69). leverage and shortNotional make the financing base explicit and auditable.

takerPerSide.description: Taker fee per side as a fraction (e.g. 0.0004 = 4 bps). Charged on every |delta position| change.

makerPerSide.description: Optional maker fee per side as a fraction, when fills are passive.

slippagePerSide.description: Optional slippage / market-impact per side as a fraction.

financing.description: Financing / borrow cost. MUST be charged on the full levered/short notional, never on 1 unit.

ratePerPeriod.description: Financing / borrow / risk-free rate charged per period (as a fraction).

chargedOnNotional.description: Must be true: financing is charged on the FULL levered/short notional. The dated-futures leak was exactly this flag being effectively false (RF on 1 unit while ~2.95x-levered).

leverage.description: Gross leverage of the book; the financing base for the long leg. The dated-futures audit ran ~2.95x.

shortNotional.description: Absolute short notional (in units of capital) that borrow is charged on, when the strategy is short or market-neutral.

currency.description: Optional quote currency the costs are denominated in (e.g. USDT).
